purpose.title: Purpose

project_scoped.title: Project-Scoped Mode
project_scoped.body:
## Project-Scoped Mode

Use `project_scoped` when the agent owns and continuously evolves the codebase, so durable knowledge accretes and pays off across many units of work.

Typical fit: greenfield, actively developed, or owned projects.

The full memory model is active:

- durable decisions go to a project-level `DECISIONS.json`,
- invariants, ownership contracts, known traps, and source-of-truth paths go to context capsules (`context-docs/`),
- backlog holds valuable work not yet committed to the active sequence,
- at the end of a branch or work unit, durable findings and decisions are PROMOTED up into those project-level homes.

The decision log is worthwhile here because future agents keep working the same project and must not silently reverse past choices.

task_scoped.title: Task-Scoped Mode
task_scoped.body:
## Task-Scoped Mode

Use `task_scoped` when work arrives as independent tickets against a codebase the agent does not own, and each ticket ships and is then forgotten (deploy-and-forget).

Typical fit: foreign or legacy business codebases worked ticket by ticket.

There is no project-owned durable home to promote into, so:

- everything durable folds into the unit-of-work active state (the ticket STATE),
- durable decisions and their rationale (why-it-is), constraints, and READ-FIRST gotchas live in STATE, not a separate `DECISIONS.json`,
- the plan stays ephemeral (next steps only, no history); once a step is executed, its reason and resulting state are persisted into STATE,
- a separate `DECISIONS.json` is NOT used: it would fragment per ticket and die when the ticket is archived,
- when the ticket closes, its STATE archives with it.

Context capsules and a project decision log are inactive in this mode. Anti-knowledge (dead ends, what NOT to do and why) is recorded, when it occurs, as a constraint inside STATE.

choosing_the_mode.title: Choosing the Mode
choosing_the_mode.body:
## Choosing the Mode

The active work profile sets the default; the project may override it in `PIPELINE-CONFIG.json`.

- `SocratexBusinessDev` (foreign / ticket-driven work): default `task_scoped`.
- `SocratexGamedev` (owned game project): default `project_scoped`.

The default is not a hard rule. A long-lived business product the agent genuinely owns may set `project_scoped`; a game built as throwaway contract work may set `task_scoped`.

Decide by ownership continuity, not by language, framework, or lifecycle stage. A legacy project can still be owned long-term (decisions accrete). A greenfield module can still be throwaway ticket work (decisions die with the ticket).

relationship_to_branch_mode.title: Relationship to Branch Mode
relationship_to_branch_mode.body:
## Relationship to Branch Mode

`branch_mode` and `memory_mode` are independent axes.

- `branch_mode` (`linear` / `branch_scoped`): WHERE active working memory physically lives while work is in progress (committed project memory, or per-branch local files).
- `memory_mode` (`project_scoped` / `task_scoped`): WHETHER durable knowledge has a home that survives the unit of work, and therefore whether the decision log and context capsules are active and whether promotion happens.

The `branch_mode` "Branch End" promotion step (promote durable findings to `context-docs/` and durable decisions to `DECISIONS.json`) applies only under `project_scoped`. Under `task_scoped` there is no promotion target: durable content stays in the unit STATE and archives with it.

A workspace may relocate the physical store (for example a central, ticket-keyed working-memory directory) without changing this contract; memory mode is about durability topology, not file location.
